next_steps: Measurement of serum immunoglobulin levels (IgG, IgA, IgM)
Dihydrorhodamine (DHR) flow cytometry assay to assess neutrophil oxidative burst
Genetic testing for CGD mutations (CYBB, CYBA, NCF1, NCF2, NCF4)
Repeat liver biopsy with specialized molecular testing (e.g., 16S ribosomal RNA gene sequencing)
Evaluation for occult biliary pathology or immunodeficiency-related malignancy

differential_diagnoses: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated GLILD and susceptibility to recurrent bacterial infections
STAT1 or STAT3 gain-of-function mutation presenting as systemic granulomatous disease
Adult-onset Chronic Granulomatous Disease (CGD) due to hypomorphic mutations
Occult gastrointestinal or biliary tract fistula resulting in recurrent polymicrobial hepatic abscesses
Multifocal Hepatosplenic Granulomatous Disease of unknown etiology (e.g., Sarcoidosis with opportunistic superinfection)
next_steps: Measure quantitative serum immunoglobulins (IgG, IgA, IgM) and lymphocyte subsets (CD19, CD27, memory B-cell phenotyping)
Perform Dihydrorhodamine (DHR) flow cytometry assay to evaluate neutrophil oxidative burst
Request genetic testing using a targeted Primary Immunodeficiency panel or Whole Exome Sequencing
Assess functional antibody responses to protein and polysaccharide antigens (vaccine titers)
Consult Immunology and Infectious Disease for comprehensive evaluation of suspected primary immunodeficiency
Perform 16S ribosomal RNA gene sequencing on existing hepatic tissue/abscess samples
Evaluate for hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) via ferritin, soluble IL-2 receptor, and sCD163 levels
